data.title: Yidu Tech Sets AGM to Approve Dividend, Board Mandates and Share-Issuance Authority
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Yidu Tech, Inc. ( (HK:2158) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Yidu Tech Inc. has convened its annual general meeting for August 28, 2026 in Beijing, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended March 31, 2026 and vote on a proposed final dividend. The agenda also includes re-election of two independent non-executive directors, renewal of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, and broad mandates for the board to issue, allot and transfer shares within defined limits, which would give management continued flexibility in capital management and potential future fundraising.

The share-issuance mandate caps new and treasury share issuances at up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, with an additional buffer linked to shares repurchased under a separate buyback authorization. These resolutions, if passed, will maintain Yidu Tech’s ability to adjust its capital structure in response to market conditions while formalizing board authority over director remuneration and auditor fees, matters of direct interest to shareholders and corporate governance observers.

More about Yidu Tech, Inc.

Yidu Tech Inc. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the technology sector, focusing on data-driven and digital solutions for healthcare and related industries. The company is governed by a board that includes independent non-executive directors and engages international audit firms, reflecting a structure aligned with global capital market practices.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$5.15B

data.title: CSPC Wins China Approval for Long-Acting Schizophrenia Injection
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group ( (HK:1093) ) has shared an update.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group has received marketing approval in mainland China for its independently developed Paliperidone Palmitate Injection, a second-generation long-acting antipsychotic indicated for both acute and maintenance treatment of schizophrenia. Available in multiple strengths, the once-monthly injection is designed to deliver rapid onset without supplemental oral medication, improving adherence and reducing relapse and rehospitalisation rates for patients with poor treatment compliance.

The product has been recommended in leading domestic and international clinical guidelines, including the 2025 Chinese Guidelines for the Prevention and Treatment of Schizophrenia, underscoring its clinical and pharmacoeconomic value. This approval expands CSPC’s psychiatric and neurological portfolio and strengthens its strategic position in the central nervous system drug market, as the company signals ongoing investment in high-value therapies to broaden treatment options for patients.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1093) stock is a Buy
with a HK$13.05 price target.

see the HK:1093 Stock Forecast page.

More about CSPC Pharmaceutical Group

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group is a Hong Kong–incorporated pharmaceutical company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group focuses on developing and marketing innovative drugs, with a growing portfolio in psychiatric and neurological therapies aimed at addressing high-burden chronic conditions such as schizophrenia.

The latest update is out from China Overseas Nuoxin International Holdings Limited ( (HK:0464) ).

China In-Tech Limited has signed a non-legally binding memorandum of understanding to explore a possible investment in CreatorX Inc, a U.S.-incorporated company developing an artificial intelligence and Web3.0-based creator economy platform. The potential deal, still subject to due diligence and further negotiations, signals China In-Tech’s interest in expanding into next-generation digital creator ecosystems, though the company has cautioned shareholders that the transaction may not proceed and pledged to update the market in line with Hong Kong listing rules.

If completed, the investment could enhance China In-Tech’s strategic positioning in AI-enabled content and Web3.0 services, potentially diversifying its technology portfolio and exposure to the global creator economy. However, with no binding terms agreed yet and the structure and size of the transaction undecided, the announcement primarily underscores early-stage exploratory discussions and highlights the associated uncertainty for investors.

Aluminum of China ( (HK:2600) ) just unveiled an update.

Aluminum Corporation of China Limited announced that its controlling shareholder, Chinalco, and concerted parties plan to increase their holdings of the company’s A and H shares by between RMB1 billion and RMB2 billion over up to twelve months, capped at 2% of total share capital. The funding will come from internal or self-raised resources of the entities implementing the increase.

To support this move and leverage regulatory policies encouraging shareholding increases, Chinalco has secured a loan commitment from Industrial and Commercial Bank of China’s Beijing branch for up to RMB1.8 billion, with a term of no more than three years, dedicated solely to boosting its A-share stake in Chalco. The initiative underscores Chinalco’s confidence in Chalco’s prospects and is positioned as a measure to protect minority investors and reinforce the company’s capital market presence.

New Provenance Everlasting Holdings Limited announced that, effective 3 August 2026, its registered office, principal share registrar and transfer agent in Bermuda will move to a new address at Richmond House, 12 Par-la-Ville Road, Hamilton HM 08. Tricor Investor Services Limited will continue to act as the company’s branch share registrar and transfer office in Hong Kong, and shareholders seeking to register share transfers in Hong Kong must continue to lodge applications with Tricor at its existing address, indicating that operational processes for Hong Kong investors remain unchanged despite the Bermuda office relocation.

The board, led by Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Ho Yu-shun, confirmed that the change primarily affects the company’s Bermuda-based corporate and administrative functions. The continuity of Tricor’s role in Hong Kong helps maintain stability and clarity for shareholders and market participants, signalling that the company is managing its corporate infrastructure update without disrupting day-to-day share registration and transfer arrangements in its primary listing market.

data.title: China Power’s June Hydropower Surge Fails to Offset First-Half Sales Dip
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) has provided an announcement.

China Power International Development reported that group electricity sales in June 2026 rose 1.45% year on year to 11.46 million MWh, driven largely by strong hydropower output, while total sales for the first half slipped 0.81% to 62.03 million MWh. The shift reflects robust growth in hydropower generation, particularly in Hunan and Guizhou, contrasted with declines in wind, coal-fired and some photovoltaic output, signaling an ongoing transition in the company’s generation mix.

Sales from wholly owned and controlled hydropower plants surged 52% in the first half, offsetting weaker contributions from coal-fired power, which fell nearly 11%, and wind power, which dropped about 14%. Major associates and joint ventures also recorded lower electricity sales, indicating broader softness in traditional and some renewable segments, a development that may weigh on overall utilization rates and underscores the company’s increasing reliance on hydropower assets for growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold
with a HK$3.30 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock,
see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development is a Hong Kong-incorporated power producer focused on generating and selling electricity across mainland China and selected overseas markets. Its diversified portfolio spans hydropower, wind, photovoltaic, coal-fired, natural gas and environmental power plants, with a notable regional presence in provinces such as Hunan, Guizhou, Sichuan and Guangxi.

data.description:

  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from KFM Kingdom Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3816) ).

KFM Kingdom Holdings has entered into a three-year contract manufacturing agreement between its subsidiary KPP(SZ) and PRC-based company HHY, effective from 24 July 2026. Under the deal, KPP(SZ) will manufacture and supply products to HHY, with pricing, quantities, specifications, and delivery details to be negotiated separately.

The arrangement constitutes a continuing connected transaction because HHY is 70% indirectly owned by Jack Sun, son of chairman and executive director Peter Sun, making HHY an associate of a connected person under Hong Kong listing rules. As the relevant transaction ratios exceed 0.1% but remain below 5%, the deal is a non-exempt connected transaction subject to disclosure and annual review, but it does not require independent shareholders’ approval, signaling manageable compliance obligations for the company and clarifying governance treatment for investors.

More about KFM Kingdom Holdings Ltd.

data.title: Qinqin Foodstuffs Issues Profit Warning as Revenue and Margins Slide
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from Qinqin Foodstuffs Group (Cayman) Company ( (HK:1583) ) is now available.

Qinqin Foodstuffs Group has warned that its unaudited revenue for the six months ended 30 June 2026 is expected to fall about 8% year-on-year to roughly RMB485 million, with profit attributable to equity shareholders plunging around 73% to 82% to between RMB4 million and RMB6 million. The company cited weaker sales through traditional retail channels amid softer consumer spending, lower sales volumes of jelly and rice wine products that reduced factory utilisation and economies of scale, and a related RMB22 million drop in gross profit and a two-percentage-point decline in gross margin, partly offset by stronger sales to snack food chains and growth in its OEM business, prompting a cautionary note to shareholders and investors as final results are still being finalised.

The company noted that the interim figures are based on preliminary, unaudited management accounts and may be adjusted before the formal results announcement scheduled for mid-August 2026. Management urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading the company’s shares pending publication of the finalised interim results, highlighting the significant profitability pressure despite some resilience from newer channels and OEM operations.

More about Qinqin Foodstuffs Group (Cayman) Company

Qinqin Foodstuffs Group (Cayman) Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed food manufacturer focused on snack foods, jelly products and rice wine, serving both traditional retail channels and snack food chains. The group also operates an OEM manufacturing business, positioning it as a contract producer for other brands within the broader packaged food sector.

Average Trading Volume: 53,284

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

data.title: China Life revamps profit distribution in connected investment partnership
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Life Insurance Co ( (HK:2628) ) has shared an announcement.

China Life Insurance Company Limited has announced a change to the profit distribution mechanism of a connected investment partnership formed with China Life Jiangsu, Jiangsu Fund and China Life Property and Casualty Insurance. The partners plan to sign a supplemental agreement by the end of 2026, shifting from project-based profit allocation to a model based on the overall performance of the partnership, aligning more closely with market practices.

Under the new structure, capital will first be returned to limited partners, then to the general partner, followed by an 8% annualized return hurdle for both before remaining excess income is split 80% to limited partners and 20% to the general partner. The board considers the revised terms fair, commercially reasonable and beneficial to shareholders, noting that several directors with roles in related entities abstained from voting on the resolution to avoid conflicts of interest.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2628) stock is a Buy

OneForce Holdings Limited has called an annual general meeting to be held physically in Beijing on 21 September 2026, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements and the reports of directors and auditor for the year ended 31 March 2026. The meeting will also consider the re-election of directors, authorization of the board to set director remuneration, and the re-appointment of KPMG as the company’s auditor.

Shareholders will vote on granting the board a general mandate to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued shares and a separate mandate to issue additional shares and related securities, subject to defined limits and regulatory requirements. data.title: China Life renews connected insurance sales pact with CLP&C
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Life Insurance Co ( (HK:2628) ) has shared an update.

China Life Insurance Company Limited plans to renew and extend its insurance sales cooperation with affiliate China Life Property & Casualty through a new Insurance Sales Framework Agreement. Under the arrangement, China Life will continue to act as an agent for CLP&C, selling a wide spectrum of property and casualty products, including compulsory, automobile and various commercial and agricultural policies, and collecting premiums in CLP&C’s name.

Because CLP&C is an associate of the controlling shareholder, the renewed arrangement is classified as a continuing connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, triggering reporting, announcement and annual review obligations but not independent shareholder approval. However, as cumulative dealings with the parent group exceed 5% of China Life’s latest audited net assets, the new framework requires shareholder approval under Shanghai Stock Exchange rules and, once approved, will replace the existing agreement from 1 January 2027, providing continuity to the group’s distribution and agency income streams.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2628) stock is a Buy
with a HK$35.00 price target.

More about China Life Insurance Co

China Life Insurance Company Limited is a leading Chinese life insurer listed in Hong Kong, providing a broad range of insurance products and related services. The company’s controlling shareholder, China Life Insurance (Group) Company, holds about 68.37% of its issued share capital, and the group maintains close operational ties with its affiliates, including China Life Property & Casualty (CLP&C).

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions

Great Harvest Maeta Holdings Limited ( (HK:3683) ) has shared an announcement.

Great Harvest Maeta Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 26 August 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and approve the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 March 2026, along with the reports of the board and independent auditor. The meeting will also consider the re-election of key executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, approval of directors’ remuneration, re-appointment of Rongcheng (Hong Kong) CPA Limited as auditor, and a general mandate authorising the board to issue additional shares, underscoring ongoing governance upkeep and potential capital-raising flexibility.

These agenda items signal the company’s intention to maintain continuity in its leadership and audit arrangements while preserving the ability to tap equity markets if needed, which may influence its future funding strategy and balance sheet management. The broad share issuance mandate, including powers over treasury shares, is standard among Hong Kong–listed firms but highlights management’s focus on retaining financial agility in a cyclical shipping industry, with implications for existing shareholders in terms of future dilution and corporate oversight.

More about Great Harvest Maeta Holdings Limited

Great Harvest Maeta Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating within the shipping and maritime transport sector with a focus on dry bulk logistics and related marine services. The group manages a fleet and associated subsidiaries that support its core shipping activities and serves regional and international cargo markets.
